_comment: NON-OPERATIONAL PLACEHOLDER SAMPLE — NOT real GAIA data. GAIA (gaia-benchmark/GAIA) is ACCESS-GATED on HuggingFace: fetching it requires accepting the dataset terms and authenticating (HF token). agenttic cannot vendor gated content it has not been granted access to, so these records are clearly-labeled, fabricated, schema-only placeholders that mirror the real GAIA validation record shape (task_id, Question, Level, Final answer, file_name) only. They exist so the GAIAAdapter, suite wiring, and the gaia_answer_match (GAIA normalized exact-match) metric can be exercised in tests/demos. To ingest the REAL GAIA validation split (with answers) into the local registry, accept the terms at https://huggingface.co/datasets/gaia-benchmark/GAIA, set HF_TOKEN, then run: agenttic standard ingest gaia --full. See ATTRIBUTION.md + LICENSE.txt.

Question: [PLACEHOLDER — not a real GAIA question. A Level-1 GAIA task asks an assistant to find a single fact via web/tool use. Stand-in: How many sides does a hexagon have?]
[PLACEHOLDER — not a real GAIA question. Stand-in string-answer task: What is the capital city of France?]
[PLACEHOLDER — not a real GAIA question. A Level-2 GAIA task chains multiple tool/reasoning steps. Stand-in list-answer task: List the first three positive even numbers in ascending order.]
[PLACEHOLDER — not a real GAIA question. Stand-in numeric-with-units task answered as a bare number: What is 5% of 200 expressed as a whole number?]

[PLACEHOLDER — not a real GAIA question. A Level-3 GAIA task requires long-horizon multi-tool reasoning, sometimes over an attached file. Stand-in string-answer task: What color do you get by mixing blue and yellow?]
